title: euphrasia collina subsp. paludosa
intro: Euphrasia collina subsp. paludosa, also known as marsh eyebright, is a subspecies of perennial plant in the gentian family, native to the wetlands of Europe and Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which help to soothe and protect the eyes, skin, and mucous membranes. The health benefits of marsh eyebright include reducing inflammation and pain, treating eye infections and conjunctivitis, and relieving respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Herbal preparations of marsh eyebright include tinctures, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
